text.title: experimentation-at-scale Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for experimentation-at-scale. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Idea-triager (sonnet): On idea intake. Input: Idea text, opportunity tree, traffic estimate. Output: Testability score + tier (A/B, prototype, qual, irreversible). Cheap models for collection and triage.
rule.rationale: Sonnet for structured task decomposition. Triage gates ideas before PM invests in hypothesis authoring.

rule.statement: Hypothesis-author (sonnet): Per candidate idea. Input: Idea, opportunity tree node, prior experiments. Output: experiment-doc.md with if-then-because, primary metric, MDE. Reject if hypothesis names a feature instead of a behavior change. Reject if MDE is missing or > likely effect by 3x. Require prior_evidence: field with at least 2 sources.
rule.rationale: Sonnet for structured authoring. Hypothesis-author must enforce behavioral framing and evidence requirements before any sample-size math runs.

rule.statement: Ice-rice-ranker (haiku): Weekly slate. Input: Hypothesis pool, eng dep graph, current OKRs. Output: Ranked slate with confidence, effort, reach, dep flags. Feed eng dep graph; ranker will otherwise ignore 6-week blockers.
rule.rationale: Haiku for ranking math. Dep awareness prevents surface of ready-to-ship ideas that actually depend on long-running work.

rule.statement: Assumption-mapper (sonnet): Pre-launch. Input: Hypothesis, surface. Output: Falsifiable assumptions + stop conditions + decision rule. Pair every experiment with a kill criterion before launch.
rule.rationale: Sonnet for structured writing. Stop-conditions make the kill threshold explicit and prevent PM bias toward shipping.

rule.statement: Readout-interpreter (opus): At decision point. Input: Ops readout JSON, hypothesis doc, segment cuts. Output: Verdict (ship/iterate/kill/extend) + caveats. Check SRM and power before reading lift. Fail fast on invalid experiments. Surface guardrail breaches BEFORE primary metric. Surface novelty risk if runtime less than 14 days OR engagement metric.
rule.rationale: Opus for verdict synthesis where misreading is expensive. Fail-fast on validity checks prevents nicely worded ship memos on broken assignments.

rule.statement: Stakeholder-narrator (sonnet): At decision point. Input: Verdict, OKR map, audience. Output: Exec memo, slack blurb, ticket update. Force verbatim copy of CI low/high; never paraphrase. Never round confidence intervals ("4% lift, 95% confidence") into clean numbers.
rule.rationale: Sonnet for narrative writing. Verbatim numbers prevent stakeholders from misinterpreting precision.

rule.statement: Learning-curator (opus): Monthly. Input: All closed experiments. Output: Opportunity-tree edits, "patterns we found", "patterns we killed". Require N>=5 with shared opportunity-tree branch before emitting a "pattern" claim. Do not hallucinate patterns from N=2 experiments.
rule.rationale: Opus for synthesis. Monthly curation links experiments back to the discovery tree, building org memory and preventing local-maxima drifts.

rule.statement: Roadmap-impact-modeler (sonnet): Quarterly. Input: Closed experiments, OKR tree. Output: Adjusted forecast for next quarter's roadmap. Emit ranges and label assumptions; never single-number forecasts. Do not compound point estimates into 4-quarter forecasts and report as fact.
rule.rationale: Sonnet for structured writing. Range forecasts prevent the illusion of precision and flag assumptions explicitly.

rule.statement: Pre-register the decision rule, not just the metric — written, signed by sponsor, archived in Notion. Without this, "we won" becomes negotiable.
rule.rationale: Decision rules encode what "victory" means before the data arrives. Otherwise, post-hoc interpretation drifts based on leadership politics.

rule.statement: Tie every experiment to one opportunity-tree node; if it doesn't link, it's a CTA test, not product work — schedule, but cap to ≤25% of slate.
rule.rationale: Opportunity-linked experiments build strategic learning. Capping CTA tests prevents drift into micro-optimizations that look productive but don't inform roadmap.

rule.statement: Keep one primary metric per experiment. PM agents will be tempted to add "secondary primary"; reject in review.
rule.rationale: Multiple primary metrics introduce p-hacking risk and bury the main hypothesis in noise.

rule.statement: Pre-register segment cuts (max 5). Post-hoc segments are exploratory only and labelled as such — never drive ship decisions.
rule.rationale: Pre-registration prevents p-hacking. Exploratory segments are valid for hypothesis generation but not for decisions.

rule.statement: Treat null results as findings, not failures. Add to learning-curator inputs; over time, a pattern of nulls in a region of the opportunity tree means rethink the opportunity.
rule.rationale: Null results rule out hypotheses and redirect discovery effort. Patterns of nulls in one branch signal the team is chasing a mirage.

rule.statement: Run a quarterly "decisions we got wrong" memo with sponsor — single highest-leverage org-learning loop.
rule.rationale: Quarterly calibration prevents the team from repeating mistakes and surfaces systemic issues (e.g., "we keep over-weighting novelty effects").

rule.statement: Cap PM time on experiment ops to less than 20%. If it's higher, the platform isn't mature enough; push work back to product-ops or invest in tooling.
rule.rationale: Experiment ops is overhead; it should not dominate PM cycles. If it does, it signals weak tooling or governance.

rule.statement: Always pair an experiment with a kill criterion before launch. PMs are biased to ship; the kill rule is the only thing that keeps the slate honest.
rule.rationale: Kill criteria make the downside explicit and prevent sunk-cost fallacy from extending dead-end experiments.

rule.statement: Maintain a living opportunity-tree map; learning-curator edits it after every closed experiment so future PMs see why a path was abandoned.
rule.rationale: Org memory prevents the same hypothesis from being re-tested in 18 months. Documented abandonment is as valuable as documented wins.
